message:
perf(fcp-streaming): zero-copy WsMessage Bytes variants (br-298cj)

crates/fcp-streaming/src/websocket.rs From<Message> for WsMessage
memcpy'd every Binary/Ping/Pong frame via .to_vec() even though the
upstream `fastwebsockets::Message` representation is already backed by
reference-counted `Bytes`. On the recv hot path every non-text frame
paid O(frame_len) memcpy + allocation.

## Fix

Switched WsMessage::{Binary, Ping, Pong} from Vec<u8> to Bytes:

  pub enum WsMessage {
      Text(String),
      Binary(Bytes),
      Ping(Bytes),
      Pong(Bytes),
      Close(Option<WsCloseFrame>),
  }

- From<Message> for WsMessage now moves Bytes directly (zero copy).
- From<WsMessage> for Message also becomes zero-copy on the send path.
- Added builder constructors WsMessage::ping(impl Into<Bytes>),
  WsMessage::pong(impl Into<Bytes>) mirroring the existing
  WsMessage::binary / WsMessage::text.
- WsConnection::send_binary signature widened from
  `impl Into<Vec<u8>>` to `impl Into<Bytes>`. Vec<u8>: Into<Bytes>
  already exists (owner-transfer, no copy), so no external caller
  needs migration.
- WsMessage::as_binary / WsMessage::json updated to use
  Bytes::as_ref() so the borrow surface is unchanged.

## Call-site migration

Pattern-match destructures (slack, mattermost connectors) compile
unchanged — Bytes has the same pattern match shape as Vec<u8>.
In-tree test literals migrated mechanically:

    WsMessage::Ping(vec![...])  ->  WsMessage::ping(vec![...])
    WsMessage::Pong(vec![...])  ->  WsMessage::pong(vec![...])

(Vec<u8>: Into<Bytes> makes this work without any allocation.)

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo check --offline \
    -p fcp-slack -p fcp-mattermost
  -> finished, no errors
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-streaming --lib
  -> 519 passed; 1 failed (ws_connection_recv_times_out_when_peer_
     blackholes_after_handshake — pre-existing flaky timing test,
     reproduces on HEAD without these changes).
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-streaming --test no_mock_integration
  -> 81 passed; 0 failed.

Closes flywheel_connectors-298cj.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Broader test/clippy verification is currently limited by unrelated dirty-tree blockers in crates/fcp-manifest/src/lib.rs and intermittent rch worker nightly availability.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api-versions","connectors","hardening"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-28a4h","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-kn1et","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:57.599223Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1950,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-28a4h","author":"jemanuel","text":"File: connectors/salesforce/src/client.rs:21
Current: const API_PATH: &str = "/services/data/v59.0";

Salesforce releases 3 API versions per year (Spring, Summer, Winter). v59.0 corresponds to Winter '24. As of 2026, the current version is likely v62.0 or higher. Older versions are eventually retired. Check https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.api_rest.meta/api_rest/dome_versions.htm

Steps:
1. Check current Salesforce REST API version
2. Update API_PATH constant
3. Make version configurable (Salesforce orgs may need to pin specific versions)
4. Add version discovery: Salesforce provides GET /services/data/ which returns available versions
5. Consider implementing version auto-negotiation in configure() if the org's API is available","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:56Z"},{"id":1977,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-28a4h","author":"GoldMill","text":"In progress: updated Salesforce default REST API version/path to 66.0/v66.0, added optional api_version configuration/normalization, surfaced resolved API version in health/doctor/readiness, and extended unit+integration coverage. Fresh rch verification is running after a final parser-hardening pass.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:10:31Z"},{"id":1980,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-28a4h","author":"GoldMill","text":"Verification update: shared HEAD now already contains the Salesforce API-version changes under commit 6f0afc9c. Fresh rch verification is being treated as post-merge validation rather than a pending local patch. Package integration tests are currently blocked by an unrelated dirty-tree compile failure in crates/fcp-manifest/src/lib.rs (ManifestTimeouts scope error).","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:13:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-28rd0","title":"TEST: fcp-webhook no-mock test expansion","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-03-05T22:20:01.539767Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-05T22:30:00.380673Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T22:25:49.313646Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":840,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-28rd0","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"fcp-webhook test expansion: 165→176 tests (+11). Added tests for path traversal (double-dot, null intermediate), header case-insensitivity, get_i64 large values, matches_type wildcards, serde roundtrips (null/complex payloads, DeliveryStatus all variants), taint flag accumulation/idempotency, EventSubscription provider filtering. All clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-05T22:25:46Z"},{"id":841,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-28rd0","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: Added 34 no-mock integration tests in tests/no_mock_integration.rs. Tests cover: full pipeline (GitHub/Stripe/Slack/Linear verify→route→DLQ), concurrent idempotency (multi-provider + same-event race), DLQ overflow/reprocessing, complex routing patterns, signature cross-verification (HMAC-SHA256/SHA1/Ed25519), taint flag propagation across all providers, payload size enforcement, IP allowlist, TTL replay lifecycle, serde roundtrip stability, deterministic signature vectors. Total: 214 tests (176 unit + 34 integration + 4 compliance), all passing + clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-05T22:30:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-292","title":"fcp.jira: JQL query execution engine","description":"# fcp.jira: JQL Query Execution Engine

## Goal
Provide a safe, typed, and ergonomic JQL query layer for search and automation.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `jira.jql.search`
- `jira.jql.validate`
- `jira.jql.autocomplete`

## Key requirements
- Capability gated (`jira.issues.read`).
- Query safety:
  - strict escaping/encoding of user-provided values
  - prevent injection into raw JQL strings where possible (prefer builder)
- Pagination:
  - deterministic iteration over `startAt`/`maxResults`
  - stable ordering requirements when paginating
- Performance:
  - request field subsets; avoid fetching full issue bodies unless required

## Tests
- Mock-only tests cover:
  - query builder correctness
  - pagination edge cases
  - invalid JQL errors
  - rate limits

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents can run complex searches reliably with predictable pagination.

Operation: jira.search_jql with pagination support. Jira connector has existing unit + integration tests.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:01:20Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-298cj","title":"[PERF] fcp-streaming::From<Message> for WsMessage: recv path copies every binary frame","description":"crates/fcp-streaming/src/websocket.rs:362-389. Converting fastwebsockets::Message into WsMessage clones every Binary/Ping/Pong payload with to_vec(), and recv() uses that conversion on the inbound hot path before handing frames to callers. Complexity is O(frame_len) extra memcpy and allocation for every non-text frame, even though the upstream representation is already bytes-backed. Proposed improvement: store binary/control payloads as Bytes (or a borrowed/owned enum) inside WsMessage so recv/send can preserve zero-copy buffers instead of round-tripping through Vec<u8>.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:35.406028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:13:11.300193Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-298cj","title":"[PERF] fcp-streaming::From<Message> for WsMessage: recv path copies every binary frame","description":"crates/fcp-streaming/src/websocket.rs:362-389. Converting fastwebsockets::Message into WsMessage clones every Binary/Ping/Pong payload with to_vec(), and recv() uses that conversion on the inbound hot path before handing frames to callers. Complexity is O(frame_len) extra memcpy and allocation for every non-text frame, even though the upstream representation is already bytes-backed. Proposed improvement: store binary/control payloads as Bytes (or a borrowed/owned enum) inside WsMessage so recv/send can preserve zero-copy buffers instead of round-tripping through Vec<u8>.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:35.406028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:18:18.156722Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:18:18.155866Z","close_reason":"Changed WsMessage::{Binary,Ping,Pong} variants from Vec<u8> to Bytes so the recv hot path (fastwebsockets::Message -> WsMessage conversion at websocket.rs:362-372) preserves the reference-counted buffer instead of memcpying into a fresh Vec<u8> via .to_vec() on every received frame. From<Message> for WsMessage now moves Bytes directly; From<WsMessage> for Message also becomes zero-copy on send. Added WsMessage::ping() and WsMessage::pong() builder constructors that accept impl Into<Bytes> (mirrors existing WsMessage::binary). WsConnection::send_binary signature changed from impl Into<Vec<u8>> to impl Into<Bytes> — internal; no external callers need migration since Bytes accepts Vec<u8> via existing From impl. Migrated 25 test literals from WsMessage::Ping(vec![...]) / Pong / Binary to WsMessage::ping(vec![...]) etc. Connectors (slack, mattermost) pattern-match data destructure unchanged (works for Bytes too). 519 lib tests pass (1 pre-existing flaky test unrelated), 81 integration tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-29zav","title":"fcp-mesh/gossip: expand unit tests (60:1 LOC:test gap)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:27:16.463125Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-04T23:29:11.688351Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T23:29:11.688336Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":712,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-29zav","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Added 26 unit tests to fcp-mesh/src/gossip.rs (31→57 total). Coverage: IbltPlaceholder (clear/encode, default), XorFilterPlaceholder (with_seed digest difference, default, may_contain empty), GossipState (list_objects, symbols_for_object, zone_id), GossipSummary (signing_bytes deterministic, with_signature), GossipRequest (is_valid_with_limits), GossipMessage serde roundtrip, GossipStats (debug/clone), GossipConfig (defaults), PeerGossipState (update_from_summary resets failures, peer_id, may_have_symbol), MeshGossip (announce_symbol admitted/quarantined, has_symbol unknown zone, list_objects_in_zone, zone_stats, create_request, find_symbol_sources, create_summary none). All 57 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-04T23:29:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2a5e","title":"[IDEA-14.2] limits usage lint/helper","description":"## Goal
Encourage connectors to reference `limits.rs` in validation logic.

## Details
- Optional lint rule or test helper to detect magic numbers for known limits.
- Documentation on how to update limits.

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least one connector example uses the limits module.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2ady","title":"[POL] Tests: unit + integration","description":"# [POL] Tests: unit + integration

## Goal
Comprehensive test suite for policy bundle workflows.

## Unit Tests
- Bundle signature verify/fail cases.
- Diff stability and risk summary correctness.
- Preview determinism and reason code stability.

## Integration Tests
- Apply a bundle and verify enforcement changes.
- Rollback restores prior behavior.

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON logs for each test stage (bundle_id, result, duration_ms).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic outputs with stable ordering.
- Clear, redacted logs suitable for audit.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.
